query_normalized: the role of cognitive dissonance in justifying unethical ai use psychology
rationale: The paper focuses on the technical architecture of creative problem-solving in AI agents (a computer science/engineering topic) and does not measure or discuss the psychological mechanism of cognitive dissonance, human unethical behavior, or the justification processes of human users. It fails all inclusion criteria (a-f) as it addresses a distinct construct sharing only the keyword "AI" while operating in a completely different domain (agent design vs. human psychology).
